items.title: 66 shots, one goal - Forest's goalscoring troubles to continue with Man City next?
items.summary: Sixty-six attempts. One goal. One team with a problem to solve as the threat of relegation looms large.

items.title: 'In de geest van Van Basten: vijf voetbalregelwijzingsvoorstellen'
items.summary: Columnist Frank Heinen heeft aanbevelingen om het voetbal te verbeteren.

items.title: Engelse lessen Gasiorowski helpen: 'Kan nu hele gesprekken voeren'
items.summary: Niet alleen binnen het veld, maar ook daarbuiten zet PSV'er Yarek Gasiorowski grote stappen. De eerste maanden was het lastig communiceren voor de Spanjaard in Eindhoven. Zijn Engelse lessen sorteren echter effect. Peter Bosz vertelde dat er tegenwoordig hele gesprekken in het Engels te voeren zijn met de verdediger.

items.title: Nagelsmann kondigt verrassingen aan in WK-selectie: 'Niet iedereen zal dat begrijpen'
items.summary: Julian Nagelsmann heeft in een interview met Kicker aangekondigd dat hij richting het WK van 2026 enkele opvallende keuzes gaat maken in zijn selectie. De bondscoach van Duitsland waarschuwt dat niet alle beslissingen op begrip zullen kunnen rekenen.

items.title: 'Slecht nieuws voor Heracles: KNVB verleent geen dispensatie voor Heilmann'
items.summary: Vincent Heilmann wordt niet de nieuwe hoofdtrainer van Heracles Almelo. Volgens De Telegraaf verleent de KNVB geen dispensatie. Dat was nodig, omdat Heilmann (29) zijn trainerspapieren nog niet heeft.

items.title: Atalanta get knocked down after tubthumping week ‘saving Italian football’ | Nicky Bandini
items.summary: Raffaele Palladino’s side lacked energy after salvaging Serie A pride last week, when league was written off prematurely On Wednesday, Atalanta “saved Italian football”. At least, that’s what the headlines said after their sensational 4-1 win over Dortmund that overturned a two-goal first-leg deficit and ensured Serie A representation in the last 16 of the Champions League. Yet on Sunday they could not save themselves from defeat to Sassuolo. This ought to have been so much easier. Their opponents were a man down from the 16th minute, when Andrea Pinamonti was sent off for a studs-up challenge on Berat Djimsiti. Perhaps Atalanta let complacency creep in, multiplied by exhaustion from their midweek exertions. Or maybe Sassuolo deserve all the credit for the resilience they have developed this season under Fabio Grosso. Continue reading...

items.title: Steijn vertrekt na dit seizoen bij Sparta
items.summary: Maurice Steijn vertrekt na dit seizoen bij Sparta Rotterdam. Gesprekken over de verlenging van het aflopende contract van de Haagse trainer hebben niet tot een akkoord geleid, waardoor de samenwerking na dit seizoen stopt.

items.title: 'Rage Room' introduced at WTA event - Henman: No cameras, private space, I like it!
items.summary: Tim Henman, Laura Robson and Naomi Cavaday have their say after a WTA tournament in Texas unveiled ‌a 'rage room' for players to take out their frustrations away from the cameras.

items.title: Set-pieces mean Premier League no longer a joy - Slot
items.summary: Liverpool manager Arne Slot says his "football heart doesn't like" the reliance on set-pieces in this season's Premier League.

items.title: Arsenal Women ‘reliant’ on parent club’s £11.9m injection despite revenue rise
items.summary: Total revenue rises to £21.5m from £15m Accounts show £22,000 profit after tax for 2024-25 Arsenal Women experienced huge growth in commercial and matchday revenue last season, but remain reliant on funds from the parent club, their latest accounts show. In the year to 31 May 2025, during which Arsenal won the Women’s Champions League and finished second in the Women’s Super League, the club nearly trebled their commercial revenue and their matchday revenue increased by 35% to almost £6m, bringing a club record profit of £22,000 after tax. Continue reading...

items.title: Mourinho: 'Over' for Prestianni if guilty of abuse
items.summary: Jose Mourinho said Gianluca Prestianni will have no future at Benfica if he is found guilty of racially abusing Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Arteta: 'Heart almost stopped' on late Raya save
items.summary: Mikel Arteta hailed David Raya's performance in Arsenal's 2-1 win against Chelsea after saying his "heart almost stopped" before the goalkeeper made a crucial stoppage time save to keep the Gunners in pole position in the Premier League title race.

items.title: United got 'Old Trafford bonus' in win - Palace boss
items.summary: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner has branded the decision to award Manchester United a penalty in their 2-1 defeat on Sunday an "Old Trafford bonus."

items.title: Tudor slams 'cheating' Fulham after Spurs loss
items.summary: Tottenham interim head coach Igor Tudor took aim at his own players before he slammed "cheating" Fulham after another defeat.

items.title: Forest latest: European efforts damaging survival bid, says Pereira
Arne Slot admits he does not enjoy watching most Premier League matches
items.summary: He points to narrowing gap and reliance on set plays Liverpool head coach will not change his team’s style Arne Slot has admitted he does not enjoy watching most Premier League matches but says he will not change Liverpool’s philosophy. The head coach feels the narrower quality gap between top and bottom and a growing reliance on set plays have reduced the entertainment. “Most of the games I see in the Premier League are not for me a joy to watch,” Slot said, “but it’s always interesting because it’s so competitive and that is what makes this league great – because there’s so much competitiveness. Everyone can win against everyone, but just as someone who loves to watch football, without being interested in who’s winning or losing it, just to be enjoyed, I think there’s a big difference now between three or four years ago in the Premier League. Continue reading...

items.title: England 'given reassurances' over safety in Turkey
items.summary: Sarina Wiegman says England have been told there are no safety concerns over their World Cup qualifier in Turkey despite the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Important goals and big saves: Who were the weekend's difference makers?
items.summary: This weekend in the Premier Leaue gave us heroics by the bucketload. Sky Sports picks out the peak performers who produced the standout moments.

items.title: Was Chermiti's overhead kick best Old Firm goal this century?
items.summary: Is Youssef Chermiti's overhead kick for Rangers against Celtic the best of the 2000s so far? Or do some of the other strikes in the fixture get your vote?

items.title: VAR review: Did Chelsea deserve penalty in loss to Arsenal?
items.summary: It appeared Arsenal's Declan Rice had intentionally hit the ball with his arm, and the call could've changed the game for Chelsea. Was the no-call correct?
